As one of 25 children, she set her sights on science is Associate Professor of Physics at the University of Yaound├⌐ 1 in Cameroon, where she is working on reducing friction on material surfaces. Her research on the mechanics of friction-and-wear processes on the molecular level has the potential to reduce the cost of energy and material losses caused by wear and can result in greater energy efficiency. Her primary goal is to make it possible to switch friction on and off in the near future, just as we can flip light switches on and off. baseline.final_answer: Friction at a molecular level is being studied to understand and manipulate processes occurring between interacting surfaces in relative motion. Research involves molecular dynamics simulations and single molecular force spectroscopy to explore energy landscapes and reveal mechanisms of friction. The goal is to potentially control friction, making it possible to switch it on and off, which could lead to reduced energy loss and greater efficiency.
